title: Variables (var, let, const)
explanation: Variables are used to store data in JavaScript. You can declare them using var, let, or const. The main difference lies in scope and reassignment behavior.
points: var is function-scoped and can be redeclared
let is block-scoped and can be reassigned
const is block-scoped and cannot be reassigned

title: Data Types
explanation: JavaScript has different types of values, broadly categorized into primitive and non-primitive types.
points: Primitive types include string, number, boolean, null, undefined, symbol, bigint
Non-primitive types include objects, arrays, and functions
typeof is used to check the data type
example:
let x = 10;           // number
let y = "hello";    // string
let z = true;       // boolean

typeof x; // "number"

title: Functions
explanation: Functions are reusable blocks of code that perform a specific task. They can accept inputs and return outputs.
points: Functions can be declared or expressed
Arrow functions provide a shorter syntax
Functions help improve code reusability

title: Scope
explanation: Scope determines where variables can be accessed in your code. JavaScript has global, function, and block scope.
points: Global scope: accessible everywhere
Function scope: accessible inside functions
Block scope: created with let and const

title: Closures
explanation: A closure is a function that remembers variables from its outer scope even after that scope has finished executing.
points: Closures retain access to outer variables
Used for data privacy and encapsulation
Common in callbacks and event handlers

title: Hoisting
explanation: Hoisting is JavaScript's behavior of moving variable and function declarations to the top of their scope before execution.
points: var is hoisted and initialized as undefined
let and const are hoisted but not initialized
Function declarations are fully hoisted

title: this Keyword
explanation: The 'this' keyword refers to the object that is executing the current function. Its value depends on how the function is called.
points: In global scope, this refers to the global object
Inside objects, this refers to the object
Arrow functions do not have their own this

title: Arrays & Objects
explanation: Arrays store ordered collections of data, while objects store data as key-value pairs.
points: Arrays use index-based access
Objects use key-based access
Array methods include map, filter, reduce

title: Event Loop
explanation: The event loop allows JavaScript to handle asynchronous operations by managing the call stack and callback queue.
points: JavaScript is single-threaded
Async tasks go to the callback queue
Event loop processes tasks after call stack is empty

title: Promises & Async/Await
explanation: Promises represent the result of an asynchronous operation. Async/await simplifies working with promises.
points: Promises have states: pending, fulfilled, rejected
then() handles resolved values
async/await makes async code easier to read
